name: VIX (CBOE Volatility Index)
summary: Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **VIX 15.98 — A CYCLE LOW ON A DAY OF REAL US-IRAN MISSILE EXCHANGE + A CHOKEPOINT-CLOSURE THREAT. THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT MARKET SIGNAL OF THE WINDOW IS THIS NON-CONFIRMATION. On 1 June the US ran self-defence strikes on Goruk/Qeshm, Iran downed a US MQ-1 and fired two ballistic missiles at US forces in Kuwait, Iran SUSPENDED the talks, and threatened to 'completely close' Hormuz — and VIX printed 15.98 (-0.4%), a cycle low, while the S&P/NASDAQ/Dow made FRESH RECORDS. The equity-vol complex is pricing the de-escalation base case as DONE while the deal is SUSPENDED (Iran), Lebanon is hot, and a chokepoint-closure threat is live. This is the cheapest convexity in the dataset: a NON-calibrated Iranian act, a Lebanon-driven rupture, or a genuine operational Hormuz closure snaps the S&P -3-5% from record highs in a session. FRAME PRECISELY: 'cheapest convexity,' NOT 'markets shrugged off the war' — the crisis-sensitive leg (oil +7% intraday, Cheniere +5%, war-risk insurance ~60x) DID re-price; only the decoupled AI/equity complex ignored it. **TRIGGER WATCH: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation** (far below at 15.98). PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **VIX SANK TO A 15.3 CYCLE LOW — PRICING THE CEASEFIRE AS DONE ON AN UNSIGNED DEAL + A LEBANON RED-LINE CROSSING. VIX 15.32 (-2.7% 1D, -28.5% 3M) is the cheapest convexity in the dataset: (a) Trump has NOT signed and the two sides describe the text incompatibly (Trump: HEU 'will be destroyed'; Fars: no destruction clause, $12bn-first), and (b) Israel CROSSED Washington's Beirut red line — the explicit binding constraint on the deal. If the next Situation Room produces a rejection, or Lebanon breaks the talks, VIX gaps to the low-20s and the S&P snaps -3-5% in a session. The complacency split persists: broad-index vol prices de-escalation while defense (ITA +10.5% MoM) bids the discrete kinetic events — both can't be right for long.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **VIX 15.75 (-3.3% 1D, -16.3% MoM, -26.5% 3M) — A FRESH CYCLE LOW ON A DAY WITH A LIVE US-IRAN BALLISTIC-MISSILE EXCHANGE. THIS IS THE COMPLACENCY TELL OF THE CYCLE. The IRGC fired one ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (intercepted, zero casualty — calibrated Ain al-Assad mode) in response to a 2nd US 'defensive' strike, and the options market printed a NEW cycle low. CNBC: 'Oil markets are betting on a swift end to the Iran war — investors may regret it.' This is the 'investors may regret it' signal made tradeable: VIX 15.75 prices the de-escalation base case as DONE, not as a probability, on a day that produced direct US-Iran kinetic contact. The convexity remains the cycle's cleanest: any escalation that breaks the 'calibrated exchange' frame (Israeli unilateral, an uncalibrated Iranian salvo, or a Beirut strike that breaks the talks) reprices VIX +6-12 pts. Both sides kept negotiating (deal hardened — Trump 'no sanctions relief', nuclear 'deadlock'), so the missile was a message, not a war restart — but the options market is pricing zero residual tail. TRIGGER WATCH: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation.** PRIOR (May 24): Updated 2026-05-24. **VIX 16.70 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (-0.4% 1D, -13.5% MoM, -20.5% 3M) — AT YEAR LOWS ON DAY 86 OF AN ACTIVE BLOCKADE. THE OPTIONS MARKET IS PRICING ZERO TAIL RISK ON THE DEAL FAILING. THIS IS EITHER A STRUCTURALLY COMPLACENT MARKET OR A MARKET WITH STRONG CONVICTION THE DEAL WILL LAND. GIVEN HELIMA CROFT'S 'FINE PRINT' FRAMEWORK AND THE FARS-TRUMP ARCHITECTURAL DISPUTE, VIX 16.70 LOOKS PRICED FOR PERFECTION. SELL VIX PUTS HERE = PICKING UP PENNIES IN FRONT OF THE DEAL-FAILURE FREIGHT TRAIN. THE ASYMMETRIC LONG-VOL SETUP REMAINS CYCLE'S CLEANEST CONVEXITY PRINT.** Per markets dossier F2 + G3: cheap tail hedges (VIX calls) have positive expected value even if the central scenario is deal-success. Three paths to VIX 22-27: (a) deal-acceptance announcement → VIX collapses to 13-15 (single-tail-event); (b) Israeli unilateral → VIX +8-12 pts; (c) Mojtaba directive crystallizes deal failure → VIX +6-10 pts. **Markets pricing the middle-positive case at high probability; the convexity is asymmetric to the downside (deal-failure case is 3-5x larger move than deal-success case).** **TRIGGER WATCH**: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation. PRIOR (May 23): VIX 16.70 MAY 22 CLOSE (-0.4% 1D, -13.5% MoM, -20.5% 3M) — CYCLE LOW, FEAR DEFLATED TO 'DEAL-DONE' NOT 'DEAL-PENDING.' STRUCTURALLY INCONSISTENT WITH THE MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW DAY 5 OF 5 + CBS PENTAGON-SOURCE MEMORIAL DAY CANCELLATION. THE ASYMMETRIC LONG-VOL SETUP REMAINS CYCLE'S CLEANEST CONVEXITY PRINT.** Friday's flat-to-down VIX print into a weekend with: Trump 'every day better' (deal-direction anchor) + Munir COAS Tehran visit (mediation escalation) + Iranian institutional counter-signal fanout (Baghaei + Ghalibaf + UN mission + Tasnim 'preparing for resuming fighting') + CBS US military Memorial Day cancellation + May 24 strike-window Day 5 of 5 + Mojtaba directive Day 2 of 14 (no walk-back) + Houthi Haifa Day 5 of 14 (no kinetic) = VIX 'should' be 22-26. Why suppressed: (1) **Deal-anticipation tape fully matured** — market priced Pakistan track at ~70-75% probability of operational sign-through; (2) **Crisis fatigue** — six binaries in 30 days that didn't deliver kinetic action have compressed event-vol bidding; (3) **AI cycle dominance** — Nvidia Q1 +200% YoY profit + KOSPI +22.8% 1M + Nikkei +2.7% 1D = vol-sellers active on AI-led risk-on; (4) Memorial Day weekend US equity holiday absorbs front-end vol bid mechanically. **The Sunday-night Asia open is the cleanest test**: any kinetic event puts VIX +5-8 pts overnight; deal-acceptance puts VIX to 13-15. **All three paths (deal delivers / Israeli unilateral / Mojtaba forecloses) have VIX at 22-27 within 14 days — meaning May 22 close of 16.70 is the cycle's cheapest convexity print.** **TRIGGER WATCH: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation.** PRIOR (May 22): **VIX 16.61 -14% 1M / -20.9% 3M — FEAR-DEFLATED TO CYCLE LOW; PRICING DEAL-ANTICIPATION AS DONE NOT RISK. STRUCTURALLY INCONSISTENT WITH THE POLICY-BIFURCATION ARCHITECTURE INTEL DESCRIBES.** Expected with Mojtaba directive foreclosing nuclear deal architecture + Bibi 'hair on fire' pushing for Israeli unilateral action + Gabbard resignation removing IC restraint + IRGC 'global offensive' threat May 21 + still-active Houthi Haifa blockade rhetoric (Day 4 of 14): VIX 'should' be 22-26. Why underpriced: (1) **Deal-anticipation dominance** — Pakistan-mediated text published in leak architecture has been treated as ~70% probability of operational sign-through by index-vol market; (2) **Crisis fatigue** — five months of intermittent escalation/de-escalation has compressed event-vol bidding (each binary that doesn't deliver kinetic action reduces the next binary's risk premium); (3) **Nvidia + AI cycle absorbing capital** — S&P/NASDAQ at/near records → index-vol bid weak (low-vol regime self-reinforcing); (4) **PPI / Warsh / yields regime** has compressed inflation-vol channel (TIPS-implied vol low). **VIX 16.61 is structurally inconsistent with the policy-bifurcation architecture intel describes.** Either (a) Pakistan deal track delivers operational sign-through within 5-10 sessions and VIX is correctly pricing the high-probability path; (b) Israeli unilateral action surfaces and VIX +8-12 pts in 24-48 hours; (c) Mojtaba directive crystallizes deal collapse and VIX +6-10 pts over 5 sessions. **All three paths have VIX at 22-27 within 14 days — meaning today's 16.61 is the cycle's cheapest convexity print.** The market has priced consensus, not optionality. **Reversibility: ASYMMETRIC — far more downside-protected than upside-pricing.** VIX-protection trade has rarely been this cheap during an active crisis cycle. **The 'VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions' trigger remains watching at 16.61** (further from threshold than May 19; this is the cycle low). PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **C7 VIX 18.43 — STILL SUB-20 ON THE STRONGEST KINETIC TAPE OF CYCLE.** Expected on a weekend that produced NYT preparation report + UAE Barakah drone strike + Saudi territorial drone intercept + Trump-Netanyahu war coordination + Trump 'clock is ticking' + Israeli security cabinet + Brent +2% gap: VIX 23-28 Monday. Why still suppressed: (i) Friday +6.8% was already the inflation-shock + Warsh transition; vol complex already absorbed the Fed-channel re-pricing; (ii) move was orderly across asset classes (no correlated panic); (iii) summer ranges typically compress; (iv) NVDA reports May 20 keeping vol-sellers active; (v) **Tuesday May 19 NSC binary** — vol traders see this as a digital event (deal or strike), not a continuous variable; positioning is asymmetric long-tail-vol, not flat-vol bid; (vi) Pakistan-Naqvi mediation Day 2 of 14 + Houthi Red Sea Day 7 UNTRIPPED + Mojtaba Khamenei Day 11 of state-media silence = coordinated quietism from Iran's patron/proxy network, suggesting escalation control not escalation. **VIX paradox is intact**: the paper-equity leg of the bifurcation has been resolved (Friday -1.2% S&P), but the vol complex has NOT capitulated yet. Either VIX is wrong (snap to 23-28 on Tuesday strike orders) or oil/Brent is wrong (retraces to $103-106 by Friday on no-strike). **VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation; currently 18.43, ~3.6 vol points from confirmation.** **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE LEVEL IS FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSE 18.43 (+6.8% 1D, +2.7% 1M, -9.2% 3M). Vol complex still SUB-20.** **TRIGGER WATCH: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation.** Per markets-dossier H reversibility map: VIX 12-14 (-22 to -33%) if MOU signs by May 21 (now near-zero probability given Trump posture); VIX 26-32 (+45 to +80%) if Hormuz remains closed through June (base case given NYT report). **If Monday May 18 open prices the NYT preparation report fully, VIX +30% spike to 23-24 is base case.** Why VIX has not capitulated: (a) move was ORDERLY across asset classes — no liquidity event, no margin call signature; (b) summer ranges typically compress (June-August seasonal vol depression); (c) NVDA earnings May 20 keeps vol-sellers active (front-end vol sold into the print); (d) systematic dealers still LONG GAMMA above 7,400 SPX — they BUY dips, suppressing realized vol. **The asymmetric long-vol setup remains the cleanest hedge structure of the cycle**: if Monday opens with NYT-priced gap-up in Brent and VIX gaps to 23-24, regime change is confirmed. **PRIOR (May 16):** VIX 18.43 (+6.8% 1D, +2.7% 1M, -9.2% 3M) — vol complex repriced into the stagflation-shock day but did NOT capitulate (still sub-20)**. Yesterday's cycle low of 17.26 was the cleanest setup of the cycle; today's resolution direction is toward crisis confirmation but not yet panic. **Why VIX stayed sub-20 despite S&P -1.24%, NASDAQ -1.54%, Dow -1.07%, 10Y +14bp**: (a) move was orderly across asset classes — bond/equity/precious-metal all sold simultaneously into the PPI+Warsh+Trump-reversal stagflation collision; (b) summer ranges typically compress; (c) NVDA earnings May 20 keeps vol-sellers active; (d) systematic dealers still long gamma above 7,400 SPX. **TRIGGER WATCH: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation.** **The asymmetric long-volatility setup that yesterday's cycle-low cycle-low VIX created has activated** — paper-equity leg of cycle-extreme bifurcation has resolved violently into crisis confirmation. **Resolution path forward**: if Iran formally rejects Trump's May 16 letter and ceasefire collapses, VIX 24-28 base case; if Shamkhani-Trump frame materializes (sanctions-for-dismantling), VIX retraces 16-17. **The Trump 2-month letter window is the binding decision-tree input for VIX through ~2026-07-16.** PRIOR (May 15): **C9 VIX 17.26 (-3.4% 1D, -5.0% 1M, -14.9% 3M) — CYCLE LOW SINCE WAR BEGAN FEB 28; PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION AT EXTREME.** Expected on a day with two new kinetic incidents (Hui Chuan + Haj Ali), an Israeli KIA inside the Litani trigger window, Trump Iran ultimatum overnight ('no longer tolerate'), and Iranian Army spokesperson Akraminia 'surprising options' rhetoric: VIX should be 22-25. Got: 17.26. **Why complacency**: Trump-Xi joint statement read as tail-risk removal; S&P/NASDAQ/Dow at record closes May 14 → MoM gamma drives systematic vol selling; pre-NVDA earnings May 20 positioning bullish risk; VIX ETF complex pushing contango trade aggressively; foreign safe-haven USD bid is the alternative hedge of choice — VIX is being short-circuited as the crisis-hedge instrument. **CLEANEST PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION READING OF THE CYCLE**: Paper assets are pricing structural-stability + tail-risk-removed + AI-cycle dominance. Physical complex (Brent +1.6% refusing to fall, VLCC TD3C still multi-week highs, kinetic incidents continuing, war risk insurance still ~8x pre-war) is pricing intact-blockade + escalation-tail-risk. **ONE SIDE RESOLVES VIOLENTLY WITHIN 5-10 SESSIONS.** Asymmetric setup: if Trump-Xi joint statement produces no operational follow-through by May 20 and Brent breaks $108 sustained 2 sessions, VIX has 5-7 points of repricing upside; if Hormuz reopens physically, VIX could compress further to 15. PRIOR (May 14): VIX 17.87 sub-18 on 1-week-clock-close day; summit-pause digital regime. **G4 VIX 17.87 (-0.7% 1D, -2.7% 1M, -13.3% 3M) — SUB-18 ON A 1-WEEK-CLOSE DAY** = on Day 7/7 of Trump's 'decide within a week' mandate, with Brent above $105 and Hormuz at 2-5 ships/day, VIX should print 22-24. Sub-18 says markets fully priced Trump-Xi pause through May 16. **If pause fails to deliver framework, VIX +30% spike to 23-24 by Monday is the base case** (markets dossier G4 + H). Trump-Xi summit Day 1: Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door; Vance softer line; Pentagon Sledgehammer naming. Forced-pause-via-Beijing hypothesis operationally validated through clock close. **Per markets dossier H reversibility map: VIX 12-14 (-22 to -33%) if MOU signs by May 21; VIX 26-32 (+45 to +80%) if Hormuz remains closed through June.** Binary digital — summit is forcible time-window where the binary collapses to a digital. PRIOR (May 13): VIX has emerged as the cleanest transmission mechanism for binary event-window pricing in the cycle — appearing in 4+ causal chains (C1, C2 direct; C5, C9 indirect). The summit-pause mechanic operationalises VIX as a digital around discrete event windows. **C1 VIX 17.99 (-2.1% 1D, -5.9% 1M, -13.6% 3M) = THE PARADOX OF MAY 13** — expected on day of Trump-rejection-1-week-clock-close + Litani-crossing + 90%-enrichment-rhetoric + Saudi-strikes-disclosure + USD/INR record: VIX +5-10% intraday. Got: -2.1%, below May 6 post-pause low, approaching pre-war territory. **Why: Trump-in-Beijing summit (May 14-15) makes a kinetic strike order operationally impossible through May 15-16. Markets are not pricing the threat universe; they are pricing the FORCIBLE DELAY of decision. Options-positioning desk reads: 48-hour vol kill via timeline mechanics.** Additional drivers: EIA forecast cut to $94.85 (TASS) institutional vote against Brent fear premium; Aramco/ADNOC quiet limited Hormuz resume (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West routing; Iran missile fire rate -92% from peak (JPost + CNN intel) = capability-vs-willingness gap publicly quantified. **Investor implication is asymmetric: if Trump returns from Beijing without an order, VIX 16-handle is plausible and Brent retraces to $97-100. If he returns AND orders, VIX +30% intraday and Brent $115-120. The summit is a binary digital, not a continuous variable. The market is positioned long-vol-cheap into a known event window.** Reversibility: total — VIX 17.99 prices NO escalation through the summit + modest probability beyond; a single tweet from Beijing or a single missile launch reprices everything within the same session. VVIX (vol-of-vol) sitting at low band; pre-CPI hedges unwinding after digestion was orderly. **VIX behaviour now central to graph because it operationalises the kinetic vs paper-disruption-vs-de-escalation trinary in a tradeable way** — the band $15-25 maps to the three branches, and the summit creates a forcible time-window where the binary collapses to a digital.

The vol complex prices the de-escalation base case as DONE while the deal is SUSPENDED (Iran), Lebanon is hot, and a chokepoint-closure threat is live. Cheapest convexity in the dataset: a NON-calibrated Iranian act, a Lebanon rupture, or an operational Hormuz closure snaps the S&P -3-5% from record highs in a session. Frame as 'cheapest convexity,' NOT 'markets shrugged off the war' — the crisis-sensitive leg (oil/LNG/insurance) DID re-price.

recent_signals.headline: **VIX 15.75 (-3.3% 1D, -16.3% MoM, -26.5% 3M) — A FRESH CYCLE LOW PRINTED ON A DAY WITH A LIVE US-IRAN BALLISTIC-MISSILE EXCHANGE. THE COMPLACENCY TELL OF THE CYCLE.** (1) **The setup**: the IRGC fired ONE ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (intercepted, zero casualty — calibrated Ain al-Assad mode) in response to a 2nd US 'defensive' strike; both sides kept negotiating (deal hardened — Trump 'no sanctions relief', nuclear 'deadlock'). On a day of direct US-Iran kinetic contact, the options market printed a NEW cycle low. (2) **The signal made explicit**: CNBC ran 'Oil markets are betting on a swift end to the Iran war — investors may regret it.' VIX 15.75 is that 'investors may regret it' thesis made tradeable — vol is pricing the de-escalation base case as DONE, not as a probability, leaving zero residual tail premium on a day that demonstrated the conflict can still produce missiles. (3) **The convexity**: any escalation that breaks the 'calibrated exchange' frame (Israeli unilateral, an uncalibrated Iranian salvo, or a Beirut strike that breaks the talks) reprices VIX +6-12 pts. The market priced consensus, not optionality — and did so at a fresh extreme. (4) **The cross-asset confirmation of complacency**: the muted market reaction IS the signal — Brent +2.4% then faded sub-$100 (~$93-96); gold FELL below $4,400 to a 2-month low; S&P +0.6% to a fresh high. **TRIGGER WATCH: VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation.**
sources: CNBC 'Oil markets are betting on a swift end to the Iran war — investors may regret it'
Markets dossier 2026-05-28 C7 / Intel dossier 2026-05-28 A4 (IRGC one calibrated intercepted missile at US base in Kuwait; both sides kept negotiating; deal hardened)

recent_signals.headline: **VIX -3.3% TO 17.82 — DIGITAL-EVENT PREMIUM BLEEDING OFF; 22-SUSTAINED TRIGGER MOVES FURTHER FROM THRESHOLD.** Yesterday's brief explicitly called Tuesday a 'digital event (deal or strike)' with VIX at 18.43 reading as the 'vol complex priced the Tuesday NSC as a binary.' Trump's postponement post moved the binary OUT of Tuesday into a 2-3 day / 'five-day' window — vol complex re-prices: digital premium that was concentrated into May 19 spreads across a longer window. VIX gaps -3.3% to 17.82, moves FURTHER from the 22-sustained trigger threshold. **The VIX move is structurally consistent with the postponement, NOT with a peace deal.** If a real framework deliverable had landed, VIX would be at 14-16. At 17.82, vol is pricing 'extension of the window,' not 'resolution of the conflict.' **The May 24 NYT-window close (Day 1 of 6 remaining yesterday → Day 0 of 5 today) is the next vol-priced binary.** Reversibility: inverts on strike orders within the window (back to 22-28); compresses further to 14-16 only on framework deliverable.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A13; Markets dossier C3
recent_signals.verification: VIX 17.82 / -3.3% 1D = CONFIRMED operational; postponement-driven vol re-pricing = CONFIRMED mechanically on news timing; trigger threshold math = CONFIRMED.

Expected on Trump-rejection-1-week-clock-close + Litani-crossing + 90%-enrichment-rhetoric + Saudi-strikes-disclosure: VIX +5-10% intraday. Got -2.1%, below May 6 post-pause low, approaching pre-war territory. **Why: Trump-in-Beijing structural ceiling — summit makes kinetic strike order operationally impossible through May 15-16; markets pricing FORCIBLE DELAY of decision, not threat universe; options-positioning desk reads 48-hour vol kill via timeline mechanics.** Additional drivers: EIA forecast cut to $94.85 (institutional vote against Brent fear premium); Aramco+ADNOC quiet limited Hormuz resume (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West routing; Iran missile fire rate -92% from peak (JPost+CNN intel). CPI 3.8% was hot but expected — no shock-vol bid.
